design_principles: Evaluator finds all 8 items within 30 seconds from landing
Every claim links to raw evidence (JSON, log, code, diagram source)
Dark slate-950 theme, consistent with existing UI components

page_hierarchy.title: Privacy Router — Overview
page_hierarchy.description: Restructured landing page. Currently a minimal card grid; redesign as an evaluator-facing overview that surfaces all 8 required items as anchored sections. Each section is a self-contained card with a clear heading and a 'dive deeper' link to the full page.

content_sections.heading: Privacy Router
content_sections.content: One-sentence value proposition + university/course badge + team name. Animated session timer (existing). LangToggle (existing).

content_sections.heading: 1. Problem & Target User
content_sections.content: Two-column layout. Left: problem statement (AI agents leak sensitive data to external LLMs — PII, business secrets, research secrets). Right: target user profile (Korean researchers, engineers, anyone using AI agents with sensitive prompts). Concrete example prompt showing before/after.

content_sections.heading: 2. Quick Start
content_sections.content: Three-step install: (1) clone repo, (2) pip install + docker-compose up, (3) open localhost:8787. Inline code blocks with copy button. Link to full install page.

content_sections.heading: 3. Why Not Big Tech?
content_sections.content: Comparison table: Privacy Router vs OpenAI Moderation vs Azure Content Safety vs local-only. Key differentiators: context-aware detection (not regex), value-harm two-axis policy, masking with hydration, open-source, runs on 3B SLM.

content_sections.heading: 4. 7-Day Usage Log
content_sections.content: Timeline visualization: 7 day-cards showing date, scenario name, sensitivity outcome, and policy action. Mini bar chart of sensitive vs non-sensitive exchanges. Link to full logs page with raw JSON access.

content_sections.heading: 5. Cost & Stack
content_sections.content: Cost comparison table: recommended stack (Ministral 3B Extractor + Gemini Flash Lite Judge) at ~$0.001/conversation vs big-tech alternatives. Local/cloud stack diagram. Hardware spec (DGX Spark).

content_sections.heading: 6. Privacy & Security
content_sections.content: Three-tier summary: (1) data never leaves device for sensitive prompts, (2) Fernet encryption for masked data, (3) SQLite local storage. Key security properties in a checklist format. Link to full page with ADR references.

content_sections.heading: 7. Demo Video
content_sections.content: Embedded YouTube/Bilibili iframe (or placeholder with thumbnail + play button if not yet recorded). Fallback: link to live demo at /demo. Below: 3 annotated screenshots showing the pipeline in action.

content_sections.heading: 8. Paper & Slides
content_sections.content: Two prominent cards: (1) 'Report / Paper' linking to docs/developments/REPORT.md or hosted PDF, (2) 'Presentation Slides' linking to generated PPTX or web slides. Below: links to GitHub repo, ADR index, ground truth dataset.

page_hierarchy.title: About — Problem, Team & Differentiation
page_hierarchy.description: Deep-dive page for problem statement, target user analysis, team info, and competitive differentiation. Linked from overview sections 1 and 3.

content_sections.heading: The Problem
content_sections.content: Extended problem statement with real-world examples. Data leak scenarios (Telegram bot sending PII to OpenAI, research agent sharing unpublished results). Statistics on AI agent data exposure. Korean researcher context (GIST, KAIST, national R&D data sensitivity).

content_sections.heading: Target User
content_sections.content: User personas: (1) Researcher using AI for paper writing, (2) Engineer using AI agents for code, (3) Enterprise user with compliance requirements. Each persona shows their pain point and how Privacy Router solves it.

content_sections.heading: Differentiation vs Big Tech
content_sections.content: Detailed comparison table with 8+ dimensions: detection method (regex vs SLM context), policy granularity (binary vs two-axis), masking approach (strip vs encrypt+hydrate), cost ($0.25/1M vs $0.001/conversation), local-first option, MCP integration, open source, Korean language support. Each row with evidence links.

content_sections.heading: Team
content_sections.content: Team member names, roles, university affiliation. Course: GenAI & Blockchain 2026.

page_hierarchy.title: Documentation Hub
page_hierarchy.description: Documentation index with sidebar navigation. Replaces current /documentation. Serves as the anchor for the docs layout with persistent sidebar.

content_sections.heading: Documentation
content_sections.content: Card grid linking to all doc subpages. Each card shows title, one-line description, and a status badge (Complete / In Progress). Quick-jump to the 8 evaluator items.

page_hierarchy.title: Installation & Execution
page_hierarchy.description: Complete setup instructions. Replaces scattered info in README and DEMO_TEST_REPORT.

content_sections.heading: Prerequisites
content_sections.content: Python 3.13+, Docker, GPU (optional for local LLM), OpenRouter API key. Version matrix.

content_sections.heading: Docker Setup (Recommended)
content_sections.content: Step-by-step docker-compose commands. Environment variables. Health check verification. Copy-pasteable command blocks.

content_sections.heading: Local Development
content_sections.content: pip install, init_db(), python -m server. Development vs production config.

content_sections.heading: Hermes / OpenClaw Demo
content_sections.content: Agent-specific setup: config files, Docker compose for Hermes/OpenClaw, Telegram bot setup.

content_sections.heading: Verification
content_sections.content: curl commands to verify each endpoint. Expected responses. Troubleshooting table.

page_hierarchy.title: Architecture
page_hierarchy.description: Existing page — enhance with Mermaid source access, zoom capability, and narrative flow. Currently has diagram gallery + component map.

content_sections.heading: Pipeline Overview
content_sections.content: Interactive Mermaid diagram (rendered inline via mermaid.js, not just PNG). Annotated pipeline: User → Extractor (TwoPhase SLM) → Judge (Two-axis) → Router → Masker/Hydrator → Response. Each node clickable to expand.

content_sections.heading: Two-Tier Stack
content_sections.content: Cloud tier (OpenRouter) vs Local tier (DGX Spark vLLM). When each is used. Cost implications.

content_sections.heading: Component Map
content_sections.content: Existing agents/ and server/ breakdown. Add: db/, config/, web/ directories. File-level links to source.

content_sections.heading: Integration Ecosystem
content_sections.content: Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, MCP server, OpenAI-compatible API. How each connects. Diagram: 03_integration_ecosystem.png.

content_sections.heading: Diagram Gallery
content_sections.content: All existing diagrams with lightbox zoom. Mermaid source (.mmd) download links. PNG exports.

content_sections.heading: Architecture Decisions
content_sections.content: Index of 4 ADRs: No-auth endpoints, public admin UI, SQLite default, deprecated test preservation. Each expandable inline.

page_hierarchy.title: Usage Logs — 7-Day Experiment
page_hierarchy.description: Web-friendly presentation of the 7-day experiment logs. This is the primary evidence page for the evaluator's usage log requirement.

content_sections.heading: Experiment Overview
content_sections.content: Summary statistics: 7 days, 7 scenarios, N=5 repetitions per scenario, total exchanges count. Date range: 2026-06-09. Environment: Hermes Agent + Privacy Router API + OpenRouter.

content_sections.heading: 7-Day Timeline
content_sections.content: Vertical timeline with 7 nodes, each expandable. Each day shows: scenario name, date, sensitivity outcome distribution (pie or bar), key finding. Days: (1) General conversation, (2) GIST library reservation, (3) Paper review, (4) GIST login, (5) Long-horizon task, (6) Mixed sensitive data, (7) Full pipeline demo.

content_sections.heading: Aggregate Statistics
content_sections.content: Charts: sensitivity detection rate by category (PII, business secret, research secret), policy action distribution (allow/mask/block), confidence score distribution. Data sourced from ground_truth.json statistics + eval results.

content_sections.heading: Day-by-Day Detail
content_sections.content: Accordion panels for each day. Each contains: environment info, exchange records with raw JSON responses, per-exchange analysis. Each JSON block has a 'Copy' button and 'View Raw' link to the .md file in the repo.

content_sections.heading: Raw Log Access
content_sections.content: Table listing all 7 log files with: filename, date, exchange count, size. Each row links to the raw .md file in the repository. Also links to: ground_truth.json, eval_aggregated.json, eval_report.html.

page_hierarchy.title: Cost & Technology Stack
page_hierarchy.description: Detailed cost analysis and stack specification.

content_sections.heading: Recommended Stack
content_sections.content: Table: Extractor (Ministral 3B, $0.10/1M tok), Judge (Gemini Flash Lite, $0.25/1M tok), Router (no LLM), Masker (no LLM). Total per-conversation estimate.

content_sections.heading: Model Evaluation Results
content_sections.content: Summary table from REPORT.md Phase 1 + Phase 2. Before/after prompt engineering. Cost-performance ranking. Link to full report.

content_sections.heading: Local Stack
content_sections.content: Hardware: DGX Spark (NVIDIA). Software: vLLM, llama-server. Models tested locally: Gemma 4 E2B/E4B/12B/26B, Granite 8B, Qwen 3.5 9B, Ministral 3B, EXAONE 33B. Performance comparison table.

content_sections.heading: Cost Comparison
content_sections.content: Privacy Router vs alternatives: OpenAI ($3-15/1M tok for GPT-4), Azure Content Safety (per-1000-requests), regex-based (free but unreliable). Privacy Router: ~$0.001/conversation with 100% detection on v2 prompt.

page_hierarchy.title: Privacy & Security Model
page_hierarchy.description: Security architecture, encryption, and privacy guarantees.

content_sections.heading: Data Flow & Boundaries
content_sections.content: Diagram showing: what stays local (passwords, load-bearing secrets), what gets masked and sent (non-essential PII), what passes through (non-sensitive). Three colored zones.

content_sections.heading: Encryption & Masking
content_sections.content: Fernet symmetric encryption for masked values. UID-based placeholder system. Contract-based hydration. MaskingRecord audit trail in SQLite.

content_sections.heading: Policy Engine (Two-Axis)
content_sections.content: Axis 1: Is masking needed? Axis 2: Is the request meaningful after masking? Decision matrix. 6 policy actions: allow, mask_and_send, route_to_local, selective_mask, prompt_user, block.

content_sections.heading: Audit & Compliance
content_sections.content: AUDIT_REPORT.md summary. Critical/High/Medium/Low findings. All Critical+High resolved. ADR links for accepted risks. Type safety improvements.

content_sections.heading: Threat Model
content_sections.content: What Privacy Router protects against (unintentional data leaks, over-sharing to cloud). What it does not protect against (adversarial prompt injection, local device compromise). Known limitations from REPORT.md Section 8.2.

page_hierarchy.title: Demo Chat
page_hierarchy.description: Existing page — functional chat interface. Add: pre-loaded example prompts showing different policy outcomes (allow, mask, block).

content_sections.heading: Live Demo
content_sections.content: Existing chat UI. Enhance with: (1) 'Try these examples' dropdown with 3 pre-built prompts (non-sensitive, PII with masking, research secret blocking), (2) privacy_router metadata display panel (existing), (3) link to 'How it works' → /docs/architecture.

page_hierarchy.title: Admin Dashboard
page_hierarchy.description: Existing page — no changes needed for evaluator requirements. Kept as-is.

usage_log_presentation.description: Three layers: summary → interactive detail → raw access. Each layer serves a different inspection depth.

each_card.styling: border-slate-800 bg-slate-900/50 with color-coded left border (green=safe, amber=masked, red=blocked)

aggregate_chart.data: sensitive vs non-sensitive exchanges per day
aggregate_chart.library: CSS-only bars (no chart library needed, keeps bundle small)

classify_response.formatted: table showing category, span, confidence, is_essential, policy_action

each_exchange.analysis: inline commentary on what was detected and why

json_display_pattern.description: How raw JSON responses are shown in the interactive layer
features: syntax_highlighted (JSON keys in blue-400, strings in emerald-400, numbers in amber-400)
copy_to_clipboard button (top-right corner)
collapsible (default collapsed for long responses, expanded for short ones)
toggle between formatted table view and raw JSON view
json_display_pattern.responsive: horizontal scroll on mobile, full width on desktop

landing_page.description: The landing / overview page is a single scroll with 8 sections. A sticky top bar shows a mini-nav of the 8 items as small pills/chips. Clicking a pill smooth-scrolls to that section. This gives the evaluator instant access to any item.

implementation.items: 8 horizontal pills with section numbers (1-8), responsive: scrollable on mobile

docs_pages.description: All /docs/* pages share a layout with a persistent left sidebar (desktop) / hamburger menu (mobile). The sidebar contains the full docs tree. A top bar has: back to overview link, page title, LangToggle.

sidebar_styling.width: w-64 (256px) on desktop, full overlay on mobile

demo_and_admin.description: Demo and Admin pages keep their existing top-bar nav (back link + LangToggle). No sidebar — these are functional pages, not documentation.

mobile_strategy.description: On mobile (<768px): sidebar collapses to hamburger, section pills become horizontally scrollable, cards stack vertically, code blocks get horizontal scroll.

design_patterns.name: SectionCard
design_patterns.description: The primary content container for each evaluator item on the overview page. A bordered card with numbered heading, content area, and a 'Learn more →' link.
design_patterns.styling: rounded-xl border border-slate-800 bg-slate-900/50 p-6 hover:border-slate-700 transition

variants.numbered: has a small blue badge with section number (1-8) in top-left
variants.highlighted: border-blue-500/50 for the most important sections (demo video, paper links)

design_patterns.name: CodeBlock
design_patterns.description: Syntax-highlighted code block with copy button. Used for installation commands, JSON responses, curl examples.
design_patterns.usage: Install page, logs page (raw JSON), architecture page (Mermaid source)
design_patterns.styling: bg-slate-900 rounded-lg p-4 overflow-x-auto font-mono text-sm relative
features: Copy button (top-right, uses navigator.clipboard.writeText)
Language badge (bash, json, python, yaml)
Line numbers (optional, for long blocks)
Word wrap toggle (optional)

design_patterns.name: AccordionPanel
design_patterns.description: Expandable panel for day-by-day log detail, ADR content, and FAQ-style sections.
design_patterns.usage: Logs page (per-day detail), architecture page (ADR inline expansion)

behavior.body: slides open with transition, shows full content
behavior.default_state: collapsed (first panel expanded on logs page)
behavior.expand_all: button to expand/collapse all panels at once

design_patterns.name: ComparisonTable
design_patterns.description: Styled table for side-by-side comparisons. Used for differentiation, cost analysis, model evaluation.
design_patterns.usage: About page (differentiation), cost page (model eval), security page (policy matrix)
design_patterns.styling: w-full text-sm, header row border-b border-slate-800, alternating row backgrounds
features: Highlight column (the recommended option gets a subtle blue-left-border)
Responsive: horizontal scroll on mobile with sticky first column
Sortable columns (optional, for model eval tables)

design_patterns.name: StatusBadge
design_patterns.description: Small inline badge showing status or outcome. Extends existing Badge component.
design_patterns.usage: Logs page (policy actions), install page (verification status), docs index (completion status)

design_patterns.name: DiagramViewer
design_patterns.description: Image container with lightbox zoom and Mermaid source toggle.
design_patterns.usage: Architecture page (all diagrams), overview page (pipeline diagram)
features: Click to open in lightbox (full-screen overlay with zoom/pan)
Toggle between PNG render and Mermaid source code
Download link for both PNG and .mmd source
Lazy loading (loading='lazy')

design_patterns.name: TimelineNode
design_patterns.description: Vertical timeline element for the 7-day log overview.

styling.node: w-8 h-8 rounded-full border-2 with color based on primary outcome
styling.card: ml-12 p-4 rounded-lg border border-slate-800 bg-slate-900/50

design_patterns.responsive: vertical on all sizes, compact on mobile

design_patterns.name: InlineJSONToggle
design_patterns.description: A component that shows a formatted table view of JSON data by default, with a toggle to switch to raw JSON view.
design_patterns.usage: Logs page (classify API responses), architecture page (response format examples)

implementation.default_view: table — category, span, confidence, is_essential columns
implementation.raw_view: pre > code block with syntax highlighting
implementation.toggle: small button pair: 'Table | JSON' in top-right of container

design_patterns.name: SectionNav
design_patterns.description: Sticky top navigation bar showing section numbers/pills. Uses IntersectionObserver to highlight the current section.

styling.container: fixed top-0 left-0 right-0 z-40 bg-slate-950/80 backdrop-blur-sm border-b border-slate-800/50
styling.pill: px-3 py-1 rounded-full text-xs font-medium transition

design_patterns.name: SidebarLayout
design_patterns.description: Shared layout for all /docs/* pages. Provides persistent sidebar navigation and consistent page chrome.

sidebar.content: logo/title at top, nav items, LangToggle at bottom
sidebar.mobile: hidden by default, shown via hamburger overlay

main.margin: ml-64 on desktop, ml-0 on mobile
main.header: sticky top bar with back link, breadcrumb, LangToggle

design_patterns.name: VideoEmbed
design_patterns.description: Responsive video embed with poster image fallback.
features: 16:9 aspect ratio container
Poster/thumbnail image when video not playing
Play button overlay
YouTube iframe or self-hosted video
Fallback: 'Video coming soon' with link to live /demo

design_patterns.name: CopyButton
design_patterns.description: Inline button that copies adjacent text to clipboard. Reusable across CodeBlock, command examples, and API key display.
design_patterns.usage: Install page, logs page, admin page (API key prefix copy)

behavior.after_copy: checkmark icon for 2 seconds, then reverts

existing_assets_to_reuse: UI components: Button, Badge, Card, Input, Select, Modal, Alert, Checkbox, LangToggle
i18n store: $lib/i18n with locale toggle, EN/KO translations
Dark theme: slate-950 bg, slate-800 borders, blue-400 accents
Static diagrams: 11 PNG files in /static/diagrams/ + 3 Mermaid .mmd sources
Usage logs: 7 markdown files in docs/experiments/logs/
Evaluation data: ground_truth.json, REPORT.md, eval.log, eval_aggregated.json
new_files_needed: web/src/routes/docs/+layout.svelte — sidebar layout for all docs pages
files_to_modify: web/src/routes/+page.svelte — restructure as evaluator-facing overview with 8 sections
web/src/lib/i18n/en.json — add ~100 new translation keys for docs pages
web/src/lib/i18n/ko.json — add ~100 new translation keys for docs pages

route_migration.note: Current /documentation and /documentation/architecture should redirect to /docs and /docs/architecture respectively. Use SvelteKit redirect in +page.ts or update all internal links.

i18n_strategy.note: All new pages need EN and KO translation keys. Follow existing flat key convention (e.g., 'docs.install.title', 'logs.day1.scenario'). The i18n store supports fallback to EN when KO key is missing.
